title: IPV6 support in Podman container
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
Need to configure ipv6 network over the podman containers. I have created one network and changed the</etc/cni/net.d/"network_name".conflist> file to add ipv6 support. Still I am not able to connect to public ipv6 inside container.

When I tried to remove the container then it will throws below error - 
ERRO[0000] Error deleting network: running [/sbin/ip6tables -t nat -D POSTROUTING -s fd00::1:8:2/112 -j CNI-04437f9ba3e1c4266f92bc82 -m comment --comment name: "ipv6test" id: "0c7cdf600901b541fcfe9b3efa5d89d548fdafe040769820e454fd0f923e56a6" --wait]: exit status 1: iptables: Bad rule (does a matching rule exist in that chain?). 

Is there any workaround to both the issue

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.podman network create ipv6test
/etc/cni/net.d/ipv6test.conflist - 
{
   "cniVersion": "0.4.0",
   "name": "ipv6test",
   "plugins": [
      {
         "type": "bridge",
         "bridge": "cni-podman1",
         "isGateway": true,
         "ipMasq": true,
         "hairpinMode": true,
         "ipam": {
            "type": "host-local",
            "routes": [
               {
                  "dst": "0.0.0.0/0"
               },
               {
                  "dst": "::/0"
               }
            ],
            "ranges": [
               [
                  {
                     "subnet": "10.89.0.0/24",
                     "gateway": "10.89.0.1"
                  }
               ],
               [
                  {
                     "subnet": "fd00::1:8:0/112",
                     "gateway": "fd00::1:8:1"
                  }
               ]
            ]
         }
      },
      {
         "type": "portmap",
         "capabilities": {
            "portMappings": true
         }
      },
      {
         "type": "firewall",
         "backend": ""
      },
      {
         "type": "dnsname",
         "domainName": "dns.podman"
      }
   ]
}

2.podman run -dt --name ubuntu --network ipv6test ubuntu
71485ae7c8eea154d0be5a5253d27d0f9fe4c76e0e0b29ba2ba7af5341f020db

3. podman exec -it ubuntu bash
ping6 2001:4860:4860::8844
ping6: connect: Network is unreachable

4. podman rm -f ubuntu

ERRO[0000] Error deleting network: running [/sbin/ip6tables -t nat -D POSTROUTING -s fd00::1:8:2/112 -j CNI-04437f9ba3e1c4266f92bc82 -m comment --comment name: "ipv6test" id: "0c7cdf600901b541fcfe9b3efa5d89d548fdafe040769820e454fd0f923e56a6" --wait]: exit status 1: iptables: Bad rule (does a matching rule exist in that chain?). 

Am i doing something wrong in configuration? Please suggest
**Describe the results you received:**


**Describe the results you expected:**
Ipv6 communication should work after changes in network cni file.
Error should not be there while remove container from host

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**
podman --version
podman version 1.9.3


**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

debug:
  compiler: gc
  gitCommit: ""
  goVersion: go1.13.4
  podmanVersion: 1.9.3
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.14.9
  cgroupVersion: v1
  conmon:
    package: conmon-2.0.17-1.module+el8.2.1+6771+3533eb4c.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.17, commit: 3c703d9f178a3a53966e1d5c03d0275ea6cb36a0'
  cpus: 2
  distribution:
    distribution: '"rhel"'
    version: "8.2"
  eventLogger: file
  hostname: ip-172-31-99-83.us-west-2.compute.internal
  idMappings:
    gidmap: null
    uidmap: null
  kernel: 4.18.0-193.el8.x86_64
  memFree: 227770368
  memTotal: 8186880000
  ociRuntime:
    name: runc
    package: runc-1.0.0-66.rc10.module+el8.2.1+6465+1a51e8b6.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev'
  os: linux
  rootless: false
  slirp4netns:
    executable: ""
    package: ""
    version: ""
  swapFree: 0
  swapTotal: 0
  uptime: 50h 53m 47.67s (Approximately 2.08 days)

  search:
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - registry.redhat.io
  - docker.io
store:
  configFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  containerStore:
    number: 0
    paused: 0
    running: 0
    stopped: 0
  graphDriverName: overlay
  graphOptions:
    overlay.mountopt: nodev,metacopy=on
  graphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  graphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: xfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "true"
  imageStore:
    number: 3
  runRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
  volumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes
**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
Looks like ping issue resolved as I had some custom nft tables and after deleting nft ping6 using ipv6 inside container is working.

**### Pending Issue**
Is there any workaround to "exit status 1: iptables: Bad rule (does a matching rule exist in that chain?)" issue.
It appeared every time when i tried to remove container.
Steps - podman rm -f container name

ERRO[0000] Error deleting network: running [/sbin/ip6tables -t nat -D POSTROUTING -s fd00::1:8:4/112 -j CNI-685ba65fbd6b7fcfbf06c5ce -m comment --comment name: "demo" id: "85f12ce7ce04c4b0d1d33113c5e691f9a4d0167075f37fe914cef50a10b67b62" --wait]: exit status 1: iptables: Bad rule (does a matching rule exist in that chain?). 
ERRO[0000] Error while removing pod from CNI network "demo": running [/sbin/ip6tables -t nat -D POSTROUTING -s fd00::1:8:4/112 -j CNI-685ba65fbd6b7fcfbf06c5ce -m comment --comment name: "demo" id: "85f12ce7ce04c4b0d1d33113c5e691f9a4d0167075f37fe914cef50a10b67b62" --wait]: exit status 1: iptables: Bad rule (does a matching rule exist in that chain?). 
ERRO[0000] unable to cleanup network for container 85f12ce7ce04c4b0d1d33113c5e691f9a4d0167075f37fe914cef50a10b67b62: "error tearing down CNI namespace configuration for container 85f12ce7ce04c4b0d1d33113c5e691f9a4d0167075f37fe914cef50a10b67b62: running [/sbin/ip6tables -t nat -D POSTROUTING -s fd00::1:8:4/112 -j CNI-685ba65fbd6b7fcfbf06c5ce -m comment --comment name: \"demo\" id: \"85f12ce7ce04c4b0d1d33113c5e691f9a4d0167075f37fe914cef50a10b67b62\" --wait]: exit status 1: iptables: Bad rule (does a matching rule exist in that chain?).\n" 
85f12ce7ce04c4b0d1d33113c5e691f9a4d0167075f37fe914cef50a10b67b62

Any ip6table rule that i have to add manually? Or I am doing something wrong.
@sumit0900 Can you provide `ip6tables -nvL` and `ip6tables -t nat -nvL` both with and without a container running?

Let me know if anything else is required

It looks like there are basically no rules present for IPv6 - most notably I am not seeing any masquerade rules to NAT the container?

@mccv1r0 Mind taking a look here?

A quick test on fedora 32 / podman 1.8.0 shows this works.   Note:  I don't have dnsname plugin so it was removed.   Also, ping6 doesn't exist in ubuntu image so I just used an image I have handy.

```
[root@snark net.d]# podman run -dt --name v6test0 --network ipv6test socksink-sudo 
0fe8633110ac22c9c8f5dfd5697f06ee88454ea4631b871c460e88d619fcc5f4
[root@snark net.d]# podman exec -it v6test0 /bin/bash 
root@0fe8633110ac:/# ping6 2001:4860:4860::8844
PING 2001:4860:4860::8844(2001:4860:4860::8844) 56 data bytes
64 bytes from 2001:4860:4860::8844: icmp_seq=1 ttl=119 time=1.69 ms
64 bytes from 2001:4860:4860::8844: icmp_seq=2 ttl=119 time=1.46 ms
^C
--- 2001:4860:4860::8844 ping statistics ---
2 packets transmitted, 2 received, 0% packet loss, time 1002ms
rtt min/avg/max/mdev = 1.460/1.577/1.694/0.117 ms
root@0fe8633110ac:/# exit
exit
Error: non zero exit code: 127: OCI runtime error
[root@snark net.d]# podman version 
Version:            1.8.0
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.12.15
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
[root@snark net.d]# 

```

Removing works too.

```
[root@snark net.d]# podman rm -f ubuntu
b2412c69e1bef6cece8a1a571404a0227d5b102eef24c8e9f1d5bf88a3294ae1
[root@snark net.d]# podman rm -f v6test0
0fe8633110ac22c9c8f5dfd5697f06ee88454ea4631b871c460e88d619fcc5f4
[root@snark net.d]# 
```

What version of cni is podman using?  There haven't been that many changes to CNI of late.
Podman v1.9.3 vendors code from Plugins v0.8.5. It looks like RHEL 8.2.1 shipped v0.8.6, though.
How to check the version of CNI and plugins?
`rpm -q containernetworking-plugins`
Oops, wrong button

rpm -q containernetworking-plugins
containernetworking-plugins-0.8.6-1.module+el8.2.1+6626+598993b4.x86_64

podman --version
podman version 1.9.3

cat /etc/redhat-release 
Red Hat Enterprise Linux release 8.2 (Ootpa)

Hello,

I'm facing the same issue like @sumit0900 on CentOS:
```bash
[root@poddy ~]# podman run -it --rm alpine /bin/ash
/ # ping -6 google.com
PING google.com (2a00:1450:400f:80a::200e): 56 data bytes
ping: sendto: Network unreachable
/ # ip ad
1: lo: <LOOPBACK,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 65536 qdisc noqueue state UNKNOWN qlen 1000
    link/loopback 00:00:00:00:00:00 brd 00:00:00:00:00:00
    inet 127.0.0.1/8 scope host lo
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    inet6 ::1/128 scope host 
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
3: eth0@if5: <BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP,LOWER_UP,M-DOWN> mtu 1500 qdisc noqueue state UP 
    link/ether 16:cb:e7:f5:63:01 brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff
    inet 10.88.0.37/16 brd 10.88.255.255 scope global eth0
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    inet6 fd00:dead:cafe::2/64 scope global 
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    inet6 fe80::14cb:e7ff:fef5:6301/64 scope link 
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
/ # ERRO[0046] Error deleting network: running [/usr/sbin/ip6tables -t nat -D POSTROUTING -s fd00:dead:cafe::2/64 -j CNI-b5cf3e62c17e41fe475ca220 -m comment --comment name: "podman" id: "250f1bcf26b584baf28a67daab1ced96dfab8e29dc47ab5399beafac790dfbde" --wait]: exit status 1: iptables: Bad rule (does a matching rule exist in that chain?). 
ERRO[0046] Error while removing pod from CNI network "podman": running [/usr/sbin/ip6tables -t nat -D POSTROUTING -s fd00:dead:cafe::2/64 -j CNI-b5cf3e62c17e41fe475ca220 -m comment --comment name: "podman" id: "250f1bcf26b584baf28a67daab1ced96dfab8e29dc47ab5399beafac790dfbde" --wait]: exit status 1: iptables: Bad rule (does a matching rule exist in that chain?). 
ERRO[0046] unable to cleanup network for container 250f1bcf26b584baf28a67daab1ced96dfab8e29dc47ab5399beafac790dfbde: "error tearing down CNI namespace configuration for container 250f1bcf26b584baf28a67daab1ced96dfab8e29dc47ab5399beafac790dfbde: running [/usr/sbin/ip6tables -t nat -D POSTROUTING -s fd00:dead:cafe::2/64 -j CNI-b5cf3e62c17e41fe475ca220 -m comment --comment name: \"podman\" id: \"250f1bcf26b584baf28a67daab1ced96dfab8e29dc47ab5399beafac790dfbde\" --wait]: exit status 1: iptables: Bad rule (does a matching rule exist in that chain?).\n" 
```

The versions of Podman and CNI are the latest ones:
```bash
[root@poddy ~]# podman version
Version:      2.0.4
API Version:  1
Go Version:   go1.13.4
Built:        Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
[root@poddy ~]# rpm -q containernetworking-plugins
containernetworking-plugins-0.8.6-1.el8.x86_64
```
Can you also provide the CNI config you're using, and `ip6tables -nvL` / `ip6tables -t nat -nvL` output?

Sure. Here is my CNI config:

```json
{
  "cniVersion": "0.4.0",
  "name": "podman",
  "plugins": [
    {
      "type": "bridge",
      "bridge": "cni-podman0",
      "isGateway": true,
      "ipMasq": true,
      "hairpinMode": true,
      "ipam": {
        "type": "host-local",
        "routes": [{"dst": "0.0.0.0/0"}, {"dst": "::/0"}],

              "gateway": "10.88.0.1"
            }

          [

              "subnet": "fd00:dead:cafe::/64",
              "gateway": "fd00:dead:cafe:0:ffff:ffff:ffff:fffe"
            }

        ]
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "portmap",
      "capabilities": {
        "portMappings": true
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "firewall"
    },
    {
      "type": "tuning"
    }
  ]
}
```

- **An interesting thing is that the connection to web sites like `google.com` works without any issues:**
```bash
/ # curl -IL6 google.com
HTTP/1.1 301 Moved Permanently
Location: http://www.google.com/
Content-Type: text/html; charset=UTF-8
Date: Wed, 05 Aug 2020 22:18:03 GMT
Expires: Fri, 04 Sep 2020 22:18:03 GMT
Cache-Control: public, max-age=2592000
Server: gws
Content-Length: 219
X-XSS-Protection: 0
```

Bump

It is likely that the ip6tables command is using nftables under the hood through a compatibility interface. Debian buster is such a system, to use the original iptables you need to switch to the `-legacy` executables. Example on debian buster that fixed the issue for me.

~~~sh
update-alternatives --set ip6tables /usr/sbin/ip6tables-legacy
update-alternatives --set iptables /usr/sbin/iptables-legacy
~~~

CNI should be able to work with the `nftables` frontend commands - we did a lot of work a year or so ago to get a good experience with `iptables` with the `nftables` backend working as expected. Unfortunately we did not work with `ip6tables` at the same time. Most of what needed to be changes was exit codes (CNI checks those to determine what a command actually did, and they seem to have changed during the transition).
